title: Chamaecrista orbiculata
taxonomy: Chamaecrista orbiculata is classified within the family Fabaceae, commonly known as the legume family. This species is further classified in the subfamily Caesalpinioideae and tribe Cercideae. Chamaecrista orbiculata is also known as the 'sandhill partridge pea' and is native to the southeastern United States. It is a small annual herb with trifoliate leaves and produces small yellow flowers. This plant is often found growing in sandy areas and along roadsides.

intro: Chamaecrista orbiculata, also known as moonseed or sensitive plant, is a leguminous vine native to tropical regions. This plant has been traditionally used in herbal medicine to treat a variety of health issues, including inflammation, fever, and digestive problems, due to its potential anti-inflammatory, antimicrobial, and antipyretic properties. The bioactive constituents of Chamaecrista orbiculata include flavonoids, alkaloids, and saponins, which are responsible for its medicinal properties.
